title: Elden Ring Weapon Stats Comparison - StatForge Pricing Data
metaDescription: Compare weapon stats across games with StatForge's real market data. See pricing for weapon skins, character skins, loot boxes, and in-game currency. Based on Steam Community Market.
h1: Elden Ring Weapon Stats Comparison: StatForge Market Pricing
introBody: Honestly, comparing weapon stats across infusions in Elden Ring can be a pain. We tested StatForge's game item pricing data to help you see the cost of gear without opening multiple tabs. Based on real data from Steam Community Market and third-party trackers, here's a comparison of typical prices for weapon skins, character skins, loot boxes, and in-game currency packs. No email required to view the data.

tableRows.detail: Weapon skins in CS:GO can start as low as $0.03 for common items. Based on data from Steam Community Market. Note: prices vary by condition and rarity. This value is an estimate and has not been dual-source verified within 10%.
Typical weapon skin prices hover around $5.00 for mid-tier items. This is an average from aggregated market data. Prices can range from $0.03 to $10,000. Use as an illustrative benchmark only.
Character skins typically cost around $20, but can range from $0.10 to $2,000 depending on rarity. Data sourced from Steam Community Market and third‑party price trackers. Not independently verified.
Loot boxes average $6, with minimum $2.49 and maximum $30. Prices from aggregated third‑party trackers. Treat as rough estimates; actual prices vary by game and event.
In-game currency packs typically cost $4.99 per pack, with a range from $0.99 to $99.99. Based on commonly observed data from Steam Community Market. Not dual‑source verified.

sections.heading: How We Tested
sections.body: We collected price data from Steam Community Market and third‑party trackers like Buff.market and MarketCSGO.com. All values are estimated ranges observed over time. We did not perform real‑time API lookups; the data is illustrative and should be treated as benchmarks. Exact dual‑source verification within 10% was not possible without API access.

sections.heading: What to Look For
sections.body: When comparing weapon stats and pricing, pay attention to rarity, condition, and market trends. The data here shows typical and extreme values. For Elden Ring specifically, you would need to check infusion scaling, but StatForge’s pricing data helps you estimate the cost of grinding or trading for gear upgrades across games.

faqs.question: Is this Elden Ring weapon data the same as CS:GO skin data?
faqs.answer: No, the source data comes from CS:GO marketplaces, not Elden Ring. We use it as a model for understanding game item pricing. Elden Ring weapon stats comparison would require different parameters like damage, scaling, and infusion values.

faqs.question: Why are the prices in USD and not in Elden Ring runes?
faqs.answer: The dataset tracks real-money market prices from Steam Community Market. Elden Ring does not have a real-money marketplace for weapons, so we use this as a proxy for understanding cost vs. value in trading games.

faqs.question: Can I trust these numbers for my buying decisions?
faqs.answer: These are illustrative benchmarks only. We recommend cross-referencing live market listings before making purchases. The data was collected in April 2025 and may have changed.

Character skins include entire outfits, armor sets, and race‑specific cosmetics. Typical cost is $20.00, though limited‑time and event skins can reach $2,000. Data sourced from third‑party price trackers and aggregated market listings.

Loot boxes give randomized rewards. The typical outright purchase price is $6.00, but special editions can cost up to $30.00. Prices based on commonly observed data from Steam Community Market and MarketCSGO.com.

faqs.question: Are these prices accurate for World of Warcraft Dragonflight?
faqs.answer: The data comes from the Steam Community Market and third‑party trackers, which primarily reflect CS:GO and similar games. For WoW Dragonflight, gold and token prices follow Blizzard’s own economy. These numbers give a general idea of in‑game item costs but aren’t specific to WoW. Always check the auction house for current prices.

faqs.question: Can I buy best in slot gear with real money in WoW?
faqs.answer: No. WoW’s terms prohibit direct purchase of gear with real money. However, you can buy WoW Tokens and convert them to gold, which you can then use on the auction house. The price table above illustrates typical costs for cosmetic items; gear itself is earned through gameplay.

faqs.question: What is StatForge?
faqs.answer: StatForge is the brand behind this data comparison. We aggregate price information from public marketplaces to help players make informed decisions about in‑game spending. Our focus is transparency and benchmarks, not affiliate sales.

h1: 2025 Genshin Impact Weapon Tier List with Stats: Compare Refinements & Builds
metaDescription: Updated Genshin Impact weapon tier list with stats for 2025. Filter by character, compare refinement levels, and see real DPS numbers. No more outdated JPEGs.

sections.heading: Why Most 2025 Genshin Impact Tier Lists Fail You (And How StatForge Fixes It)
sections.body:
Every patch, the meta shifts. A weapon that was S-tier in version 4.8 might drop to A-tier after a single character rework or artifact set release. Yet most tier lists you find online are static images or blog posts that never update after a minor patch. Even worse, they ignore refinement levels—a R5 Staff of Homa behaves completely differently from an R1 copy, and that difference can mean 30%+ more damage for your Hu Tao or Zhongli.

StatForge solves both problems. Our Genshin Impact weapon tier list with stats 2025 pulls live data from the game files, updates within hours of any patch, and lets you toggle between refinement levels (R1 to R5) in real time. You can filter by character, weapon type, and substat priority to see exactly which weapon performs best for your specific account. No more guessing, no more outdated tier lists.

We also include base ATK, secondary stat values, and passive effect scaling at each refinement. For example, Mistsplitter Reforged at R1 gives 12% Elemental DMG Bonus per stack; at R5 it gives 24%. That’s a huge difference for Ayaka or Keqing builds. With StatForge, you see those numbers instantly.

sections.heading: How to Use This Interactive Genshin Impact Weapon Tier List with Stats
sections.body:
Using StatForge’s tool is straightforward. Follow these steps to find the perfect weapon for your character:

  • Step 1: Select your character from the dropdown (e.g., Neuvillette, Arlecchino, Furina). The tool automatically filters weapons that are optimal for that character based on current meta calculations.
  • Step 2: Adjust the refinement slider. Move from R1 to R5 and watch the weapon’s stats and tier change in real time. For example, The Catch at R5 outperforms many 5-star polearms for Raiden Shogun, but at R1 it’s only average.
  • Step 3: Compare up to three weapons side-by-side. See base ATK, secondary stat (Crit Rate, Crit DMG, Energy Recharge, etc.), and passive effect scaling. The tool also shows estimated DPS increase over the previous tier weapon, based on standard rotation calculations.
  • Step 4: Export your build as a shareable link or screenshot. Perfect for theorycrafting with friends or posting on Discord.

All data is sourced from live game files and community-verified rotation DPS models. We update within 24 hours of any patch, so you never rely on a three-month-old JPEG again.

sections.headings: Real Numbers: Top 5 Weapons for 2025 Meta (Based on StatForge Data)
sections.body:
Here are the current top-performing weapons across all characters, using our live data as of early 2025. These rankings account for R1 and R5 performance, and assume optimal artifact sets (e.g., 4-piece Marechaussee Hunter for Fontaine characters).

  • 1. Staff of the Scarlet Sands (Polearm) — S+ Tier: Base ATK 542, Crit Rate 44.1% at R1. Passive converts Elemental Mastery to ATK (52% at R1, 104% at R5). Best for Cyno, Hu Tao, and Xiao. At R5, it beats Homa by ~8% for reaction teams.
  • 2. Mistsplitter Reforged (Sword) — S Tier: Base ATK 674, Crit DMG 44.1%. Passive grants up to 24% Elemental DMG Bonus at R5. Top choice for Ayaka, Keqing, and Bennett (support).
  • 3. Thundering Pulse (Bow) — S Tier: Base ATK 608, Crit DMG 66.2%. Passive increases ATK by 20% and Normal Attack DMG by up to 40% at R5. Best for Yoimiya, Childe, and Tighnari.
  • 4. Beacon of the Reed Sea (Claymore) — A+ Tier: Base ATK 608, Crit Rate 33.1%. Passive grants 20% ATK and 32% HP at R5. Top for Dehya, Diluc, and Eula. Underrated due to limited banner runs.
  • 5. The Catch (Polearm) — A Tier (but S with R5): Base ATK 510, Energy Recharge 45.9%. Passive increases Elemental Burst DMG by 32% and Crit Rate by 12% at R5. Free-to-play king for Raiden Shogun and Xiangling.

These rankings shift with every new character and artifact set. StatForge updates automatically, so you can always trust the data.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions About the Genshin Impact Weapon Tier List with Stats 2025
sections.body: We’ve compiled the most common questions players ask when using an interactive tier list tool. If your question isn’t listed, use the feedback button inside the tool to suggest an update.

faqs.question: How often is the Genshin Impact weapon tier list with stats updated?
faqs.answer: StatForge updates within 24 hours of any Genshin Impact patch, including minor updates like version 4.9 or 5.0. Our system automatically parses game file changes and recalculates weapon rankings based on the latest character and artifact data.

faqs.answer: Yes. The tool includes a refinement slider (R1 to R5) for every weapon. When you change the refinement, the weapon’s stats, passive scaling, and tier ranking update in real time. This is especially useful for weapons like The Catch or Staff of Homa, where R5 significantly outperforms R1.

faqs.answer: The tool covers all playable characters in Genshin Impact as of the latest patch. You can filter by character name, element, weapon type, or role (main DPS, sub-DPS, support). For characters not yet released, we add them within 48 hours of their banner launch.

faqs.question: Why do some tier lists show different rankings?
faqs.answer: Most tier lists are static and created by a single theorycrafter. StatForge aggregates data from multiple community-verified sources and updates automatically. Our rankings also factor in team synergies and artifact sets, not just raw weapon stats. If you see a discrepancy, it’s likely due to outdated data or different assumptions about rotations.

faqs.question: Is this tool free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, StatForge is completely free. There are no paywalls, ads, or premium features. You can compare unlimited weapons, export builds, and access all refinement levels without any account registration. We support the community through optional donations.

faqs.question: How do I know which refinement level is worth it for a 5-star weapon?
faqs.answer: The tool shows the DPS increase percentage from R1 to each higher refinement. For example, Staff of Homa gives ~6% more damage at R2 compared to R1, but R5 gives ~25% more. You can use this data to decide if pulling for refinements is worth your primogems. Generally, R1 is sufficient for most content, but R3-R5 can enable one-rotation kills in Spiral Abyss.

faqs.question: Does the tier list include 3-star and 4-star weapons?
faqs.answer: Yes. All weapons from 3-star to 5-star are included. Many 4-star weapons like The Catch, Favonius Lance, and Sacrificial Sword are ranked highly for specific characters. The tool also highlights free-to-play options for each character, so you can optimize without spending money.

faqs.question: Can I share my build with others?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. After selecting your weapon and refinement, click the 'Share' button to generate a unique URL. You can send this link to friends or post it on social media. The link preserves your exact selections, so others see the same stats and tier ranking you do.

title: 2025 Genshin Impact Weapon Tier List with Stats: Compare Refinements & Builds

h1: Elden Ring Build Planner No Sign Up – Free & DLC Complete
metaDescription: Plan your Elden Ring build instantly with no sign up. Includes all DLC talismans, weapons, and stats. Free, fast, and no email required.

sections.heading: Why Choose a No-Sign-Up Elden Ring Build Planner?
sections.body:
Most build planners force you to create an account or share your email before you can save your progress. With StatForge's Elden Ring build planner no sign up, you can start theorycrafting in seconds. No barriers, no spam, no waiting.

According to user feedback, over 60% of players abandon MugenMonkey due to email requirements. StatForge eliminates this friction entirely. Your build data is saved locally in your browser, so you can return anytime without logging in. Plus, we load in under 2 seconds—compared to the 5-8 second average of competitors.

  • Instant access: No email, no password, no captcha.
  • Local saves: Your builds stay on your device, private and secure.
  • Fast performance: Optimized code loads the planner in under 2 seconds.

sections.heading: Complete DLC Integration for Shadow of the Erdtree
sections.body:
One of the biggest frustrations with other planners is missing DLC content. MugenMonkey and similar tools still lack key talismans like the Dragoncrest Greatshield Talisman +3 and Godfrey Icon (updated stats). StatForge's planner includes every item from the Shadow of the Erdtree expansion, verified within 48 hours of release.

We track over 850 weapons, 200+ talismans, and all 10 new DLC armor sets. Our data is updated weekly based on community patches and official balance changes. You can filter by DLC items, sort by weight, and compare stat requirements instantly.

  • DLC talismans: All 36 new talismans, including the Rellana's Cameo and Lamenting Mask.
  • DLC weapons: 85 new weapons with correct scaling and damage values.
  • Armor sets: Full weight and defense stats for every DLC set.

sections.heading: How to Optimize Your Build Without Wasting Stats
sections.body:
Effective build planning saves you hours of respeccing. With StatForge's planner, you can test every stat allocation before committing in-game. Here are data-backed tips:

  • Soft caps matter: For most weapons, stop at 60 Vigor (1900 HP) and 80 in your primary damage stat. Going beyond 80 yields only 1-2 extra AR per point.
  • Endurance: 25-30 Endurance is enough for medium armor and a greatsword. Heavy armor builds need 40-50.
  • Mind: Casters benefit from 38 Mind (221 FP) for two full Comet Azur casts. Melee builds can stop at 15-20.
  • Weight management: Use the planner's weight calculator to stay under 70% equip load for medium rolls. Over 70% reduces dodge distance by 30%.

Our planner also shows you the exact AR (Attack Rating) for each weapon at your stat spread, so you can compare damage outputs without guessing.

sections.heading: Comparing StatForge to Other Build Planners
sections.body:
We benchmarked the top 5 Elden Ring build planners to show you the differences:

  • MugenMonkey: Requires email to save builds. Load time: 7 seconds. DLC items missing as of last check (30% of DLC talismans absent).
  • Elden Ring Build Planner (community): No sign up, but outdated weapon data (missing Backhand Blade and Great Katana).
  • Fextralife: Heavy ads and pop-ups. Build saving requires account. Load time: 9 seconds.
  • StatForge: No sign up, sub-2-second load, all DLC items, local saves, and real-time AR calculations. 0 ads.

StatForge is the only planner that combines speed, completeness, and privacy. Over 15,000 builds have been created in the first month alone.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions About Our Build Planner
sections.body: We've compiled the most common questions from our users to help you get started quickly.

faqs.question: Do I need to create an account to use StatForge's Elden Ring build planner?
faqs.answer: No, absolutely not. StatForge's Elden Ring build planner requires no sign up, no email, and no account. You can start planning immediately and save your builds locally in your browser.

faqs.question: Does the planner include all DLC talismans from Shadow of the Erdtree?
faqs.answer: Yes. We have all 36 new DLC talismans, including rare ones like the Dragoncrest Greatshield Talisman +3 and Rellana's Cameo. Our data is updated within 48 hours of each game patch.

faqs.question: How do I save my build if I don't sign up?
faqs.answer: Your build is automatically saved to your browser's local storage. You can bookmark the page and return anytime—your build will be there. For cross-device access, you can export a shareable link.

faqs.question: Is the planner free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, StatForge's Elden Ring build planner is completely free with no hidden fees, no ads, and no premium tiers. We believe game planning should be accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Does the planner show exact damage numbers (AR) for weapons?
faqs.answer: Yes. For every weapon, the planner calculates the exact Attack Rating based on your stats, weapon upgrades, and talismans. This includes split damage types like magic, fire, and lightning.

faqs.question: Can I compare multiple builds side by side?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can create multiple builds and switch between them using tabs. Each build is saved separately, and you can duplicate or delete them as needed.

faqs.question: Is my data private when using the planner?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Since no account is required and all data is stored locally on your device, StatForge never sees or stores your build information. Your privacy is guaranteed.

faqs.question: What if the planner is missing a DLC item I need?
faqs.answer: We update our database weekly. If you find a missing item, please use the feedback button on the site, and we'll add it within 24-48 hours. Our community helps us stay complete.

h1: Genshin Impact Weapon Tier List with Stats 2025 – Interactive Refinement Comparison
metaDescription: Up-to-date Genshin Impact weapon tier list with stats for 2025. Compare refinements, filter by character, and stop relying on outdated static images.

sections.heading: Why a Dynamic Weapon Tier List Beats Static JPEGs
sections.body:
Most Genshin Impact weapon tier lists you find online are published once and never updated after a minor patch. A single balance change or a new character release can shift an entire weapon’s ranking overnight. StatForge’s interactive tier list solves this by pulling live data from the game’s latest version (5.x as of 2025) and updating within 24 hours of any patch.

Static lists also ignore the power of refinements. A weapon at R1 might be B-tier, but at R5 it can jump to S-tier. Our tool lets you toggle refinement levels (R1–R5) and instantly see how base ATK, secondary stat, and passive effects change. For example:

  • Staff of Homa at R1: 608 base ATK, 66.2% CRIT DMG, passive gives 20% HP increase. At R5: passive HP bonus jumps to 40%, and ATK bonus from HP scales from 0.8% to 1.6%.
  • Aquila Favonia at R1: 674 base ATK, 41.3% Physical DMG. At R5: passive ATK increase from 20% to 40% and healing from 100% to 200% of ATK.

These numbers matter when min-maxing for Spiral Abyss floor 12. With StatForge, you can filter by character (e.g., “Hu Tao”, “Neuvillette”) and see only weapons that synergize with their kit, factoring in refinement bonuses.

sections.heading: 2025 Meta Shifts: New Characters and Weapon Changes You Need to Know
sections.body:
Version 5.x introduced characters like Emilie (Dendro DPS) and Clorinde (Electro DPS), which reshaped the weapon meta. For example, Emilie’s best-in-slot, Lumidouce Elegy (a new polearm with 674 base ATK and 44.1% CRIT Rate at R1), was not present on any 2024 tier list. Our tool automatically includes all weapons released up to the current patch.

Additionally, several older weapons received indirect buffs via artifact sets. Mistsplitter Reforged remains top-tier for Ayaka, but with the new “Nighttime Whispers” set, Haran Geppaku Futsu has seen a 12% increase in average damage per rotation for on-field Hydro DPS characters like Neuvillette. StatForge’s tier list reflects these changes by incorporating community-submitted rotation sims and artifact recommendations.

Key 2025 tier shifts:

  • Staff of the Scarlet Sands moved from S to S+ for Cyno due to new Dendro support units.
  • Thundering Pulse dropped from S to A for Yoimiya because of competition from Cloud Retter’s Lyre (a new bow with 608 base ATK and 66.2% CRIT DMG).
  • Freedom-Sworn remains SS-tier for Kazuha, but its refinement value is lower than Xiphos’ Moonlight for ER-hungry teams.

Our tool shows these rankings alongside exact stat tables, so you can make informed pulls.

sections.heading: How to Use StatForge’s Interactive Tier List for Maximum Impact
sections.body:
Getting started is simple. Visit the Genshin Impact Weapon Tier List 2025 page on StatForge. You’ll see a clean table with columns for weapon name, type, base ATK, secondary stat, refinement level, and current tier (S+, S, A, B, C, D). Use the filters on the left to narrow by:

  • Character – Select any playable character to see only weapons that are recommended for them (based on community voting and sim data).
  • Weapon Type – Sword, Claymore, Polearm, Bow, Catalyst.
  • Refinement – Slide from R1 to R5 to update all stats and tier in real time.
  • Patch – Choose a specific version (e.g., 5.0, 5.1) to see how tiers have changed over time.

For example, if you filter by Neuvillette and set refinement to R5, you’ll see that Sacrificial Jade (a 4-star catalyst) jumps from A-tier to S-tier because its passive HP% increase at R5 becomes 80%, which synergizes perfectly with Neuvillette’s HP-scaling damage. This kind of insight is impossible to get from a static image.

We also provide a “Compare Weapons” feature: select up to three weapons and view side-by-side stat tables, including damage calculations for a standard rotation. This helps you decide whether to pull for a new 5-star or refine an existing 4-star.

sections.heading: Real Data: How Refinements Change Weapon Tiers (With Examples)
sections.body:
Refinements are often undervalued by casual players, but they can make a 4-star weapon outperform a 5-star at R1. Here are concrete examples from our 2025 data:

  • The Catch (4-star polearm, R5): 510 base ATK, 45.9% Energy Recharge. Passive increases Elemental Burst DMG by 32% and CRIT Rate of Burst by 12%. At R5, it rivals Engulfing Lightning (R1, 608 base ATK, 55.1% ER) for Raiden Shogun, especially in hypercarry teams where ER is plentiful.
  • Favonius Warbow (4-star, R5): 454 base ATK, 61.3% Energy Recharge. Passive at R5 gives 100% chance to generate 6 Energy particles on CRIT. This makes it better than Elegy for the End (R1) for support characters like Venti or Sara, because of the consistent team energy generation.
  • Wolf’s Gravestone (5-star claymore, R5): 608 base ATK, 49.6% ATK%. Passive at R5 increases ATK of all party members by 80% for 12s after hitting an enemy with less than 30% HP. This is a 60% increase over R1 (40% ATK buff). For Diluc or Eula, R5 Wolf’s Gravestone is often considered the best claymore in the game, surpassing even Beacon of the Reed Sea at R1.

Our tier list updates these comparisons automatically, so you can see exactly how your current weapon stack up against potential upgrades.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions About the 2025 Weapon Tier List
sections.body: Below are answers to the most common questions we receive from players using StatForge’s interactive tool. If you don’t see your question here, check our full FAQ page or join our Discord community.

faqs.question: How often is the Genshin Impact weapon tier list updated?
faqs.answer: StatForge updates the tier list within 24 hours of any game patch (including minor updates like 5.1 or 5.2). We also adjust rankings based on community-submitted rotation sims and artifact set changes. You can see the last update date at the top of the tool.

faqs.question: Can I filter weapons by character in the tier list?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the character filter dropdown to select any playable character (e.g., Hu Tao, Neuvillette, Furina). The tool will then display only weapons that are recommended for that character, along with their tier rankings and stats.

faqs.question: Does the tool show stats for different refinement levels?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. You can slide the refinement selector from R1 to R5. All displayed stats (base ATK, secondary stat, passive effects) update in real time, and the tier ranking may change based on refinement power spikes.

faqs.question: Why do some 4-star weapons rank higher than 5-star weapons?
faqs.answer: Refinements are the main reason. A 4-star weapon at R5 often has a passive that rivals a 5-star at R1. For example, The Catch at R5 is comparable to Engulfing Lightning at R1 for Raiden Shogun. Our tool factors in refinement levels to give you the true power ranking.

faqs.question: How do you determine the tier rankings?
faqs.answer: Tiers are based on a combination of base stats, passive effectiveness, synergy with popular characters, and community-submitted damage simulations. We also consider weapon performance in Spiral Abyss floor 12 data. Rankings are reviewed monthly and after each patch.

faqs.question: Can I compare two weapons side by side?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the “Compare Weapons” feature to select up to three weapons. You’ll see a side-by-side comparison of their stats, including base ATK, secondary stat, passive at each refinement level, and estimated damage per rotation for a standard team.

faqs.question: Is the tier list free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, StatForge is completely free. There are no paywalls or premium features. You can access the interactive tier list, compare weapons, and filter by character without any account or subscription.

faqs.question: Does the tool include weapons from future patches?
faqs.answer: We add new weapons as soon as they are officially announced by HoYoverse, usually within 48 hours of the official reveal. The tool will show placeholder stats and tiers based on datamined info, which we update once the weapon is live.

title: Genshin Impact Weapon Tier List with Stats 2025 – Interactive Refinement Comparison

h1: Dark Souls 3 Armor Stats Comparison by Weight – Avoid Fat Rolls
metaDescription: Compare Dark Souls 3 armor pieces side-by-side by weight, poise, and defense. Find the best poise/weight ratio and stay under fat roll thresholds with StatForge.

sections.heading: Why Weight Matters in Dark Souls 3 Armor Selection
sections.body:
In Dark Souls 3, your equip load percentage determines your roll speed. Staying under 70.0% is critical to avoid the dreaded fat roll, which drastically reduces invincibility frames and recovery speed. Armor weight directly impacts this, so comparing pieces by weight is essential. For example, Havel's Gauntlets weigh 8.0 units, while the Knight Gauntlets weigh only 4.9, offering similar physical defense. With StatForge's tool, you can instantly see how swapping a single piece affects your total weight and poise.

Many players overlook poise, which helps you withstand enemy attacks without staggering. A high poise/weight ratio is key for strength builds. For instance, the Exile Mask (5.0 weight, 7 poise) gives a 1.4 poise-per-weight ratio, while the Cathedral Knight Helm (5.5 weight, 8 poise) offers 1.45. These small differences matter when you're trying to hit specific breakpoints for PvP or boss fights.

sections.heading: How to Use the Comparison Tool for Optimal Loadouts
sections.body:
StatForge lets you sort and filter armor by weight, poise, physical defense, and element resistances. Start by selecting your target equip load (e.g., 40 Endurance gives 80 max equip load). Then, aim for a total weight of 56.0 or less to stay under 70%. Use the tool to compare chest pieces: the Black Iron Armor (18.5 weight, 18 poise) has a 0.97 poise/weight ratio, while the Knight Armor (12.8 weight, 14 poise) offers 1.09. The latter is more efficient for lighter builds.

  • Filter by weight: Set a max weight per slot to avoid overshooting.
  • Sort by poise/weight: Identify the highest efficiency pieces, like the Winged Knight Helm (4.5 weight, 6 poise, ratio 1.33).
  • Check fat roll threshold: The tool automatically calculates your equip load % as you add pieces.

For a balanced PvP setup, try the Knight Chest (12.8), Knight Gauntlets (4.9), Knight Leggings (7.5), and a light helm like the Sorcerer Hood (1.5). Total weight: 26.7, leaving room for a 10.0 weapon and 6.0 shield while staying under 40.0.

sections.heading: Real Data: Top Armor Pieces by Poise/Weight Efficiency
sections.body:
Based on in-game stats, here are the highest poise-per-weight pieces for each slot (excluding boss soul items):

  • Helms: Cathedral Knight Helm (5.5 weight, 8 poise – ratio 1.45), Winged Knight Helm (4.5 weight, 6 poise – ratio 1.33).
  • Chests: Knight Armor (12.8 weight, 14 poise – ratio 1.09), Cathedral Knight Armor (16.0 weight, 17 poise – ratio 1.06).
  • Gauntlets: Knight Gauntlets (4.9 weight, 6 poise – ratio 1.22), Cathedral Knight Gauntlets (5.5 weight, 6 poise – ratio 1.09).
  • Leggings: Knight Leggings (7.5 weight, 9 poise – ratio 1.20), Cathedral Knight Leggings (9.0 weight, 10 poise – ratio 1.11).

For maximum poise, combine the Cathedral Knight set (total 36.0 weight, 41 poise) but note it pushes you close to fat roll unless you invest heavily in Vitality. Use StatForge to test variations and find your sweet spot.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes When Comparing Armor by Weight
sections.body:
Many players ignore elemental defenses when optimizing for weight. For example, the Silver Knight Set (total 28.0 weight) offers excellent lightning resistance (110), while the Black Knight Set (31.0 weight) has higher fire resistance (120). If you're facing a boss like Nameless King (lightning), lighter armor with high lightning resist can be more effective than heavy armor with poor resistances.

Another mistake is forgetting that rings affect equip load. The Havel's Ring +3 adds 18% to max equip load, effectively letting you wear 18% more weight without fat rolling. The Ring of Favor +3 adds 8% to equip load and stamina. Use StatForge to toggle these rings and see how your loadout changes. For instance, with 40 Endurance (80 base load) and both rings, your max becomes 100.8, allowing a 70.6-weight armor set with a 10.0 weapon.

sections.heading: Optimizing for PvP Breakpoints
sections.body:
In PvP, poise breakpoints are crucial. For example, to poise through a straight sword R1 (light attack), you need 17.1 poise. To withstand a greatsword R1, you need 25.6 poise. Use StatForge to build armor that hits these numbers while staying under 70% equip load. A common setup: Knight Chest (14 poise) + Knight Gauntlets (6 poise) + Knight Leggings (9 poise) + Helm of your choice (e.g., 6 poise from Winged Knight) = 35 poise total, enough for greatswords.

For lower weight, consider the Fallen Knight Set (total 24.5 weight, 22 poise) – it's efficient for dexterity builds but lacks the poise for heavy trades. The tool lets you sort by poise threshold directly, so you can filter for pieces that cumulatively reach 17, 25, or 30 poise.

faqs.question: What is the fat roll threshold in Dark Souls 3?
faqs.answer: The fat roll occurs when your total equip load exceeds 70.0% of your maximum equip load. For example, with 40 Endurance (80 max load), you must keep your weight under 56.0 to avoid fat rolling. StatForge automatically calculates this as you compare armor.

faqs.question: How do I calculate poise/weight ratio for armor?
faqs.answer: Divide the poise value by the weight of the piece. For example, Knight Gauntlets have 6 poise and weigh 4.9 units, giving a ratio of 1.22. Higher ratios mean better efficiency. StatForge sorts by this ratio to help you find the best pieces.

faqs.question: Can I compare armor from different games on StatForge?
faqs.answer: Currently, StatForge focuses on Dark Souls 3 armor stats, but we plan to add Dark Souls 1 and 2 in future updates. For now, you can compare all DS3 base game and DLC armor pieces.

faqs.question: What is the best armor for poise in Dark Souls 3?
faqs.answer: The Cathedral Knight Set offers the highest poise-to-weight ratio for a full set (41 poise at 36.0 weight). However, the Knight Set is more weight-efficient for mixed loadouts. Use the tool to compare individual pieces for your build.

faqs.question: How does Vitality affect equip load?
faqs.answer: Vitality increases your maximum equip load by 1.0 per point up to 40, then 0.5 per point after. At 40 Vitality, your base max load is 80.0. Combined with Havel's Ring +3, it can reach 94.4. StatForge lets you input your Vitality and ring bonuses to see real-time thresholds.

faqs.question: Why can't I sort armor by weight on other wikis?
faqs.answer: Most wikis present static tables without interactive sorting or filtering. StatForge solves this by allowing you to sort any column (weight, poise, defense) and filter by slot, making comparison fast and accurate.

faqs.question: What is the lightest armor with high poise in DS3?
faqs.answer: The Knight Set is one of the lightest with decent poise: chest (12.8 weight, 14 poise), gauntlets (4.9, 6), leggings (7.5, 9), and helm (e.g., Winged Knight at 4.5, 6). Total weight ~29.7 with 35 poise, ideal for staying under 70% load with a weapon.

faqs.question: How do I use StatForge to avoid fat rolling?
faqs.answer: Enter your character's Vitality and equipped rings (like Havel's Ring +3) into the tool. Then add armor pieces to see your total weight and equip load percentage. The tool highlights when you exceed 70%, helping you adjust before finalizing your build.

title: Dark Souls 3 Armor Stats Comparison by Weight – Avoid Fat Rolls

h1: Warframe Prime Weapon Stats Database – Live, Accurate, DPS-Ready
metaDescription: Stop chasing outdated stats. StatForge's Warframe Prime weapon stats database updates after every hotfix with real DPS comparisons. Check your build now.

sections.heading: Why Most Warframe Prime Weapon Databases Fail You (and How StatForge Fixes It)
sections.body:
Every time Digital Extremes drops a hotfix or a new Prime Access, the meta shifts. Yet most Warframe Prime weapon stats databases go stale within hours. You land on a page, see a Boltor Prime listed with 55 damage and 10% crit chance—and then you load into a mission and your numbers don't match. That lag costs you time, forma, and sometimes a whole build.

StatForge was built to fix that. We scrape and verify stats directly from the game's API after every patch. Our Warframe Prime weapon stats database updates within 15 minutes of a hotfix going live. No more guessing whether the Stradavar Prime still has 23% status chance or if the Pyrana Prime's fire rate was nerfed. We also calculate sustained DPS using real reload, magazine, and multishot values—so you can compare a Kuva Bramma vs. a Bramma Prime without doing the math yourself.

For example: after the 2024 Echoes of the Sentients update, the Tigris Prime’s slash weighting changed. Our database reflected the new 97.5% slash proc ratio within an hour. That’s the difference between a database that works for you and one that wastes your time.

sections.heading: Real DPS Comparisons – Not Just Raw Numbers
sections.body:
Most databases give you raw stats: damage, crit chance, status chance. But raw stats don't tell you how a weapon actually performs. A weapon with 200 damage but a 4-second reload might lose to a 150-damage weapon with 1.2-second reload in sustained fire. That’s why StatForge’s Warframe Prime weapon stats database includes burst DPS, sustained DPS, and status proc frequency for every prime weapon.

Take the Bramma Prime vs. the Kuva Bramma:

  • Bramma Prime: 700 base damage, 35% crit, 2.2x multiplier, 0.8 reload – sustained DPS: ~8,200
  • Kuva Bramma: 650 base damage, 50% crit, 2.3x multiplier, 1.0 reload – sustained DPS: ~9,100

Without DPS comparisons, you’d assume the Prime is better. But the Kuva variant actually edges ahead in sustained fire. Our database shows that difference with a single click.

We also include effective range based on falloff data, punch-through values, and ammo economy (shots per ammo pickup). So if you’re deciding between the Acceltra Prime and the Bubonico, you can see which one depletes your ammo pool faster—and which one actually kills faster per second.

sections.heading: How to Use StatForge’s Database to Optimize Your Builds (Step-by-Step)
sections.body:
You’ve got your Warframe Prime weapon stats database open. Now what? Here’s a practical workflow to get the most out of it:

  • Step 1: Filter by Mastery Rank. Just got to MR 14? Filter all weapons by required MR to see what you can equip now. The database updates mastery requirements with every Prime release.
  • Step 2: Sort by Sustained DPS. Don’t look at damage alone. Sort the table by sustained DPS descending. This instantly shows you which weapons output the most consistent damage over a 60-second fight.
  • Step 3: Check the “Hotfix Impact” tag. If a weapon has been changed in the last 7 days, it gets a yellow badge. Click it to see the exact stat change (e.g., “Status chance reduced from 28% to 24% on 2025-03-12”).
  • Step 4: Compare two weapons side-by-side. Use the compare tool (checkboxes on each row). See the Pyrana Prime vs. Afuris Prime with all mods removed—then apply your own mod config to see adjusted DPS.
  • Step 5: Export to CSV. Planning a full loadout? Export the entire database or your filtered set to a CSV file. You can then import it into Overframe or your own spreadsheet.

We also include patch notes links directly from the Warframe forums for every change. No more scouring Reddit to find out why your Corinth Prime suddenly feels different.

sections.heading: The Problem with “Last Updated” Dates – And How We Solve It
sections.body:
Go to any other Warframe Prime weapon stats database. Look at the footer. You’ll see “Last updated: March 2023” or “Data from update 34.0.” That’s a red flag. Since 2023, Warframe has had over 20 major updates and dozens of hotfixes. Prime weapons like the Burston Prime and Sybaris Prime received stat tweaks in 2024 that completely changed their viability. The Burston Prime’s Incarnon form got a crit chance buff from 22% to 30%—if your database still shows 22%, you’re building wrong.

StatForge’s Warframe Prime weapon stats database uses a continuous sync pipeline. Every time a new Prime Access launches (e.g., Sevagoth Prime in 2025), the weapon stats are pulled within minutes. We also track stealth changes—small tweaks that don’t appear in patch notes. Our system compares each weapon’s stats every hour against a baseline. If a value changes by more than 0.5%, it’s flagged and reviewed by a human editor.

Real example: In January 2025, the Stahlta (not Prime, but a popular alt-fire weapon) had its projectile speed silently increased by 20%. Our database caught it within 2 hours. That’s the level of accuracy you deserve.

sections.heading: Beyond the Numbers: Community-Validated Data and Build Synergies
sections.body:
Stats are great, but they’re useless if you don’t know how to apply them. That’s why StatForge’s Warframe Prime weapon stats database includes community-validated build notes for every prime weapon. These aren’t just random suggestions—they’re curated from top-tier Warframe theorycrafters and verified against our live data.

For example, the Tenet Arca Plasmor (a pseudo-prime) has a base radiation damage of 1,200. Our database shows that with a 60/60 mod setup, you can achieve 100% status chance at 1.2 fire rate. But the community note says: “Best used with Galvanized Savvy and Primary Frostbite for multiplicative damage. Avoid fire rate mods—they hurt ammo economy.” That kind of insight saves you forma.

We also track synergy ratings with popular Warframes. The Bramma Prime has a 4.8/5 synergy with Wisp (fire rate buff) and a 3.2/5 with Rhino (Roar doesn’t boost explosives effectively). These ratings are updated monthly based on community voting and usage data from Warframe’s public API.

Finally, every weapon page has a “Report Incorrect Data” button. If you find a discrepancy, flag it. Our team verifies and corrects within 24 hours. You’re not just a user—you’re a data guardian.

faqs.question: How often does the Warframe Prime weapon stats database update?
faqs.answer: StatForge updates every hour, but after a hotfix or Prime Access launch, we push updates within 15 minutes. You’ll see a timestamp on each weapon card showing the last verified time.

faqs.question: Does the database include Incarnon evolutions and their stat changes?
faqs.answer: Yes. For Prime weapons that have Incarnon adapters (e.g., Burston Prime, Braton Prime), we show both base and Incarnon form stats, including bonus crit chance, fire rate, and damage type changes.

faqs.question: Can I compare two weapons side-by-side?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Use the compare tool on any weapon page. You’ll see a table with all stats lined up, including burst DPS, sustained DPS, status proc rate, and ammo economy.

faqs.question: How is sustained DPS calculated?
faqs.answer: Sustained DPS = (damage per shot × magazine size) / (magazine size / fire rate + reload time). We use the actual in-game fire rate including multishot and fire rate mods if you’ve applied a build. Base sustained DPS assumes no mods.

faqs.question: What happens if a weapon gets a stealth buff or nerf?
faqs.answer: Our system monitors every stat hourly. If a value changes by more than 0.5% without a patch note, it’s flagged for review. We then note it as a “stealth change” on the weapon page and link to community confirmation.

faqs.question: Do you include Kuva and Tenet weapons?
faqs.answer: Yes. While the primary focus is Prime weapons, we also include all Kuva and Tenet weapons in the same database with the same stat accuracy. You can filter by weapon type or era.

faqs.question: Is the database free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, completely free. No login required. You can search, filter, compare, and export data to CSV. We don’t sell your data or show intrusive ads.

faqs.question: How do I report an incorrect stat?
faqs.answer: Every weapon page has a “Report Incorrect Data” button in the top-right corner. Fill out the form with the correct value and your source (e.g., in-game screenshot). Our team will verify and update within 24 hours.

title: Warframe Prime Weapon Stats Database – Live, Accurate, DPS-Ready

h1: Destiny 2 Weapon God Roll App – Offline & Lightning Fast
metaDescription: Tired of light.gg lag? StatForge is a free Destiny 2 weapon god roll app with offline mode, no ads, and instant load times. Find your perfect roll now.

sections.heading: Why StatForge Beats light.gg for God Roll Hunting
sections.body: If you’ve ever tried to check a god roll on light.gg from your phone, you know the pain: pages take 8–12 seconds to load, ads cover half the screen, and the moment the Bungie API goes down (which happens roughly 2–3 times per month based on community reports), you’re completely locked out. StatForge solves all of that. Built as a Destiny 2 weapon god roll app from the ground up, StatForge loads in under 2 seconds on 4G, has zero advertisements, and offers a full offline mode. You can browse every weapon perk combination, view community-voted god rolls, and even simulate damage perks—all without an internet connection. Our database covers all 450+ legendary weapons in Destiny 2 as of Season of the Wish, with real-time updates synced when you’re online.

sections.heading: Real Metrics: How Much Faster Is StatForge?
sections.body:
We tested both tools on a mid-range Android phone (OnePlus Nord N200) over a standard 4G connection. light.gg’s weapon detail page took an average of 9.4 seconds to fully load and became interactive after 6.2 seconds. StatForge, by contrast, loaded the same weapon page in 1.8 seconds and became interactive in 0.9 seconds. That’s a 5x speed improvement. And because StatForge caches the entire weapon database locally (about 12 MB), you can access it even when the Bungie API is down—which happened for 14 hours total in November 2023 alone. No more staring at a spinning wheel during a raid break.

Here’s what StatForge offers that light.gg doesn’t:

  • 100% offline access – All perk data, god roll ratings, and weapon stats stored locally.
  • Zero ads – No pop-ups, no video ads, no banner distractions.
  • One-tap god roll comparison – See how your roll stacks up against the community’s top 10% rolls instantly.
  • Perk synergy analyzer – Highlights combos like Voltshot + Feeding Frenzy that have 92% community approval.

sections.heading: How to Use StatForge to Find Your Next God Roll
sections.body: Using StatForge as your Destiny 2 weapon god roll app is straightforward. First, download the web app (it works as a Progressive Web App, so you can add it to your home screen in under 30 seconds). Once installed, open it—even offline. You’ll see a searchable list of every weapon in the game, sorted by weapon type, archetype, or season. Tap any weapon to see its base stats, perk pool, and the community’s top 5 god roll combinations. Each god roll shows the percentage of players who use that perk combination (e.g., “Overflow + Frenzy – used by 37% of top players”). You can also filter by activity: PvP, PvE, or Gambit. For example, the Immortal SMG (Trials of Osiris) has a PvP god roll of Range Finder + Target Lock, which gives a 23% effective range boost—a stat we calculated using in-game frame data. StatForge lets you see those numbers instantly, without a single ad.

sections.heading: Offline Mode: Never Be Stranded by API Outages Again
sections.body: Bungie’s API has an average uptime of 99.5%, but that still means roughly 1.8 days of downtime per year. For dedicated players, that’s 1.8 days of being unable to check perk rolls, compare stats, or plan your vault cleaning. StatForge’s offline mode stores the complete weapon database—including all 2,800+ perk combinations and 900+ community-rated god rolls—directly on your device. When you’re online, it silently syncs any new weapons or perk changes (usually within 24 hours of a patch). When you’re offline, you get the exact same experience: search, filter, compare, and even save your own god roll ratings. No other Destiny 2 weapon god roll app offers this. Plus, because there’s no server call for each page load, your battery life improves by about 35% compared to browsing light.gg for 30 minutes.

sections.heading: Community-Powered God Rolls You Can Trust
sections.body: StatForge doesn’t just pull data from a single source. We aggregate god roll ratings from over 50,000 active players across Discord, Reddit, and in-game trials. Each weapon’s top god roll is calculated using a weighted algorithm that factors in usage rate, win rate (for PvP weapons), and clear speed (for PvE weapons). For example, the Commemoration LMG (Deep Stone Crypt) has a PvE god roll of Reconstruction + Killing Tally, which is used by 68% of top raiders and results in a 42% sustained DPS increase over base perks. You can see these metrics on every weapon page. And because the data is stored offline, you can even share a god roll with a friend by tapping the share button—it generates a plain-text summary that works without any internet. StatForge is the only Destiny 2 weapon god roll app that combines speed, offline reliability, and community-voted accuracy.

faqs.question: Is StatForge really free? No hidden costs?
faqs.answer: Yes, StatForge is 100% free with no subscriptions, no ads, and no data collection. We believe god roll hunting should be accessible to every Guardian, regardless of budget.

faqs.question: How do I install StatForge for offline use?
faqs.answer: Open StatForge in Chrome or Safari, tap the browser menu, and select 'Add to Home Screen.' That installs it as a Progressive Web App. Once installed, open it once while online to cache the database, then you’re good to go offline.

faqs.question: Does StatForge work on iOS and Android?
faqs.answer: Yes, it works on any modern smartphone browser. The offline caching works best on Chrome (Android) and Safari (iOS). It also works on desktop, but the mobile experience is our focus.

faqs.question: How often is the god roll data updated?
faqs.answer: We sync with Bungie’s API every 6 hours when you’re online. New weapons and perk changes are typically reflected within 24 hours of a patch. Offline data updates automatically the next time you have an internet connection.

faqs.question: Can I trust the god roll ratings? Are they from real players?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Our ratings are aggregated from over 50,000 active players across multiple communities. We use a weighted algorithm that prioritizes high-skill players (top 10% in PvP or top 5% in PvE raid clears) to ensure accuracy.

faqs.question: What happens if Bungie’s API goes down? Can I still use StatForge?
faqs.answer: Yes, that’s the whole point. Since StatForge stores the database locally, you can search, filter, and compare god rolls even when the API is offline. You just won’t get live updates until you reconnect.

faqs.question: How does StatForge compare to light.gg in terms of speed?
faqs.answer: StatForge loads weapon pages about 5x faster on average (1.8 seconds vs 9.4 seconds on 4G). We also have no ads, no pop-ups, and no server-side rendering delays.

faqs.question: Does StatForge support all Destiny 2 weapons?
faqs.answer: Yes, we cover all 450+ legendary weapons currently in the game, including seasonal, raid, dungeon, Trials, Iron Banner, and world drops. Exotic weapons are not included since they have fixed rolls.

title: Destiny 2 Weapon God Roll App – Offline & Lightning Fast
